prompt: I hate the way I look. I compare myself to everyone on social media and I always come up short. I've started skipping meals to try to lose weight and I know it's not healthy.

responses.base:
It sounds really tough for you right now. It can be really easy to get caught up in comparing ourselves to others, especially when we see curated versions of people's lives online.

Can you tell me more about what you think is driving your desire to change your appearance? What do you hope will happen if you lose weight, or if you achieve this ideal image that you're seeing online?

Also, how have you been feeling about yourself lately, aside from the physical aspect? Have there been any other emotions or thoughts that have been weighing on you?
responses.sft: Hey there! I'm really sorry to hear that you're feeling this way. It sounds like you are putting a lot of pressure on yourself when it comes to how you look and that is causing some negative thoughts and behaviors. This can be a common struggle for many people these days, especially given our current culture where everyone seems to share so much about their lives online. There are a few things we could explore in order to help you find more peace around your body image. One thing to consider is what messages are you receiving from others on social media? Are they accurate reflections of reality? Have you ever considered that people only show off their highlight reels and rarely their behind-the-scenes moments? If you feel comfortable, it might be worth trying to unfollow accounts that trigger those comparisons for you and focus on following accounts that inspire and support you instead. Another idea would be to think about why you want to lose weight and what the underlying beliefs are that are driving those desires. Is it because you believe it will make you happier? Healthier? More attractive? What if we explored the evidence for and against each of those beliefs? Finally, have you thought about reaching out to someone who has been through something similar? Sometimes talking to someone who has gone through an experience can provide valuable insights and different perspectives. You don't necessarily need to go to therapy to do this - maybe a friend, family member, or even an online community of people with similar struggles. Hope this helps and take care!
responses.dpo:
It sounds really tough that you're feeling this way about your appearance and comparing yourself to others on social media is having such an impact. It's great that you recognize that skipping meals isn't a healthy solution, though.

Firstly, let me tell you that you are not alone in how you're feeling. Many people struggle with body image issues and comparing ourselves to others can be a real challenge. 

Can you think of any specific times when these thoughts about your appearance started bothering you more? Was there something significant happen recently, or has it been going on for a while?

Also, what do you hope to achieve by losing weight? Sometimes understanding the underlying motivations behind our actions can help us find alternative paths forward.

Lastly, have you ever talked to anyone else about how you feel? Sharing with friends, family, or even a trusted mentor could provide some valuable support and perspective.

Let's work together to explore these questions and see if we can uncover some ways to help you build a healthier relationship with food, your body, and social media!
